title: Divine Council and Angelic Hierarchy
description: This theme covers the biblical concept of the divine council—the heavenly assembly of angelic beings over which Yahweh presides as King. It includes the hierarchy of angels (ranks such as Seraphim, Cherubim, Thrones, etc.), the roles of angels as messengers, warriors, and governors of nations, and the distinction between good and fallen angels. Episodes discussing the divine council, angelic ranks, and the fall of angels belong here.

title: Fall of Angels and Demonic Rebellion
description: This theme explores the various angelic rebellions, including the fall of Satan and the Watchers, the origin of demons, and the nature of demonic activity. It covers the 'five(ish) falls of angels,' the devil's rebellion after creation, the Nephilim as offspring of fallen angels, and the ongoing spiritual warfare. Episodes on the fall of angels, demons, and Halloween as a military parade are central.

title: Theosis and Salvation as Deification
description: This theme covers the Orthodox understanding of salvation as theosis—becoming partakers of the divine nature and being adopted as sons of God. It contrasts this with Western legal models of atonement and emphasizes participation in God's life through Christ. Episodes on theosis, adoption, and the nature of salvation are included.

title: Nephilim, Giants, and the War Against Giants
description: This theme focuses on the biblical and historical origins of giants (Nephilim), their connection to fallen angels, and the ongoing war against them (Gigantomachy). It includes the offspring of fallen angels and human women, the giant clans in Canaan, and how Christ's exorcisms continue this war. Episodes on Nephilim, giants, and the Sethite hypothesis are key.

title: Sacred Geography, Temple, and Creation
description: This theme explores the concept of sacred geography—places where God's presence is revealed—and the understanding of creation as temple building. It covers the Garden of Eden as a temple, the tabernacle and temple as microcosms, and the relationship between physical and spiritual geography. Episodes on sacred geography, creation, and the temple are included.

title: Body, Materiality, and the Ancient Concept of Body
description: This theme covers the ancient understanding of 'body' as a nexus of powers and potentialities, applied to God, angels, and humans. It includes the distinction between flesh and body, the garments of skin after the Fall, and the resurrection body. Episodes on the concept of body, God's body, and the nature of materiality are central.

title: Sacrifice, Worship, and Atonement
description: This theme explores the nature of sacrifice in the ancient world, the biblical understanding of worship as sacrifice, and the Day of Atonement. It covers how worship was fundamentally about feeding gods, the shift to proper worship of Yahweh, and the atonement as cleansing and restoration. Episodes on sacrifice, atonement, and the Eucharist are included.

title: Saints, the Theotokos, and the Queen Mother
description: This theme covers the veneration of saints and the Virgin Mary, including the office of queen mother in Judah and its fulfillment in Mary. It discusses the intercession of saints, the distinction between veneration and worship, and the role of Mary as Theotokos and queen mother. Episodes on Mary, saints, and relics are central.

title: Spiritual Warfare and Baptism as Recruitment
description: This theme covers the ongoing battle between God and his faithful angels/saints against fallen angels. It emphasizes baptism as recruitment into God's army, the defeat of demons through Christ, and the role of Christians in spiritual warfare. Episodes on spiritual warfare, Halloween, and baptism are included.

title: Astrology, the Heavens, and the Gospel in the Stars
description: This theme explores the ancient Israelite understanding of the sun, moon, and stars as angelic beings, the zodiac associated with the twelve tribes, and the idea that the heavens proclaim the gospel. It includes the Star of Bethlehem and the ecliptic in Psalm 19. Episodes on Jewish astrology, the gospel in the stars, and the zodiac are central.

title: Idolatry, Pagan Gods, and the Fallen Angels
description: This theme covers the nature of pagan idolatry as an inversion of creation, the identity of pagan gods as fallen angels, and the biblical critique of idolatry. It includes the Tower of Babel, the succession myth, and the death of the gods. Episodes on idolatry, pagan gods, and the gods as fallen angels are included.

title: Davidic Kingship and Messianic Rule
description: This theme covers the Davidic king as an icon of God's rule, the office of queen mother, and the messianic fulfillment in Christ. It includes the connection between David's throne and God's throne, and the role of the king in establishing justice. Episodes on Davidic kingship, the queen mother, and Christ as King are central.

title: Mystery of Christ and the Incarnation
description: This theme explores the incarnation and virgin birth as mysteries hidden from angels, the role of Christ as the unique Son of God, and the cosmic significance of his coming. It includes the Star of Bethlehem, the genealogies, and the fulfillment of Old Testament types. Episodes on Christmas, the incarnation, and the mystery hidden from angels are included.

title: Old Testament Narratives and Patriarchs
description: This theme covers the stories of key Old Testament figures such as Abraham, Melchizedek, Moses, and Elijah, and their theological significance. It includes the promises to Abraham, the figure of Melchizedek, and the prophetic ministry of Elijah. Episodes on Abraham, Melchizedek, and the patriarchs are central.
